chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: so guys today is our day 76  and in day 76 i am going to give you an exercise  in which you have to manipulate pdf files  and you have to do this by using pypdf module  so we have a module named pypdf

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: with the help of which you can merge pdf files  so let's say i gave you 5-6 pdf files  you can merge them in one  and if you want you can add some other functionality to it  but here you have to make the merging functionality

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: so what you have to do is  let's say you have 7 pdf files  so how to merge those 7 pdf files in one pdf  you have to write this program  now you will ask in which order you have to merge

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: so let's do one thing  we will maintain the alphabetic sorting  and according to that you can do the sorting  but if you want to take a particular type of pdf file  then you can take that too

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: but for now we will keep this question simple  i want you to explore pypdf  which is a very amazing open source  pure python pdf library  you can do splitting, merging, cropping and encrypting pdf files

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: i have used it a lot and i have given projects to many clients  i have earned a lot of money from this library  because when someone has to make a piece of software  and they are only interested in their work  like i had a client

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: he made a pdf file  it was a big thing for him to merge 3 pdf files  and it happens that if this module is not there  and we write 3-4 lines of code in python  then things become easy for us

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: but for that person this is a big thing  i make a bat file which invokes my python file  i install python in the client's computer  or i make an api for it  and i host that api on a server

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: and i tell it that you have to keep this server up  or if you don't want to keep that server up  then i install python in their computer  and make an automated script for it  or there is a thing called portable python

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: which becomes a whole exe of python  but for now you have to solve this exercise  and whoever solves it i will give him a shoutout  so guys i am making this video from rapnet's mobile app  and i am actually traveling

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: and the schedule is very busy  but i don't want to miss 100 days of code  so i am making this video from rapnet's mobile app  so guys do explore pypdf  there are things written in its documentation

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: if you go in its documentation then you will learn a lot of things  i am opening the read the docs of pypdf in my phone  and you can see here  that pypdf2 is a free and open source library  there are many examples in it

chunks.title: Exercise 8 - Merge the PDF
chunks.text: so at least merging is a very simple thing  you can solve this question  and i want you to solve it  so guys thank you so much for watching this video  and i will see you next time
